message:
refactor(net/tcp): replace bare Waker vec with ID-tracked AcceptWaiter and introduce AcceptFuture

The previous AcceptWaiters implementation stored a flat Vec<Waker> and
relied on will_wake() comparisons to avoid duplicates. This had two
problems: (1) a cancelled future could leave a stale waker in the vec
indefinitely, and (2) re-polls from the same task required a linear
scan to find the matching waker.

This change introduces:

- AcceptWaiter struct pairing a monotonic u64 ID with its Waker, so
  each registered interest is individually addressable.
- AcceptWaiters::register() now takes a &mut Option<u64> waiter_id
  parameter: on first poll it allocates a new ID, on subsequent polls
  it updates the existing waker in-place via clone_from() if the task
  changed, avoiding both duplicates and stale entries.
- AcceptWaiters::remove(id) enables precise cleanup when a waiter is
  cancelled (swap_remove for O(1) removal).
- AcceptFuture<'a> is a new Future type returned by TcpListener::accept
  that encapsulates the poll loop (try non-blocking accept, register
  interest on WouldBlock, handle storm backoff and fallback rewake)
  with a Drop impl that calls remove() on its waiter_id, ensuring no
  leaked waker entries after cancellation.

wake_all() and wake_others() now go through waiter.waker instead of
bare Waker values, preserving existing thundering-herd mitigation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
